author: Dr. Tomas Reyes (Emergency Medicine) / Dr. Priya Anand (Medicine PGY-2) / Dr. Helena Vargas (Acute Care Surgery)
department: Emergency Department / Internal Medicine / Acute Care Surgery
content:
ADMISSION HISTORY AND PHYSICAL — GREATER BALTIMORE MEDICAL CENTER
Date/Time: 6/10/2027, 1:00 PM (ED arrival 10:50 AM)
Patient: Pierce, Dorothy A (MRN: MRN-90215922)
DOB: 11/8/1954 (Age 72)

CHIEF COMPLAINT: Fevers, chills, and right-upper-quadrant/epigastric abdominal pain.

HISTORY OF PRESENT ILLNESS:
Ms. Pierce is a 72-year-old woman with a history of GERD, type 2 diabetes mellitus, and poorly controlled hypertension who presents with approximately 18 hours of progressively worsening right-upper-quadrant and epigastric abdominal pain associated with fevers and shaking chills. The pain became severe and constant overnight, with associated nausea and two episodes of non-bilious emesis. She measured a temperature of 101.5 F at home. She drove herself partway then called her daughter, who brought her to the ED.

She denies chest pain, palpitations, dyspnea, or syncope. No prior episodes of irregular heartbeat or known arrhythmia. No prior abdominal surgery. No melena or hematochezia.

PAST MEDICAL HISTORY (per PCP records, Dr. Robert Feldman, Towson Internal Medicine Associates):
- Hypertension, poorly controlled (on three agents: lisinopril, amlodipine, hydrochlorothiazide; home systolics frequently 150-168)
- Type 2 diabetes mellitus (metformin; most recent HbA1c 7.6%)
- GERD (omeprazole)
- Hyperlipidemia (atorvastatin; LDL at goal)
- Osteopenia (calcium/vitamin D)
- Grief/adjustment reaction after spousal loss (2025; improving)

NOTABLY: NO known coronary artery disease, NO prior stroke or TIA, NO peripheral arterial disease, NO heart failure. NO prior atrial fibrillation. No prior echocardiogram on file.

PAST SURGICAL HISTORY: None.

MEDICATIONS (home, per reconciliation with patient + daughter):
- Lisinopril 20 mg daily
- Amlodipine 10 mg daily
- Hydrochlorothiazide 25 mg daily
- Metformin 1000 mg BID
- Atorvastatin 40 mg QHS
- Omeprazole 20 mg daily
- Calcium carbonate/vitamin D3 BID
- Fish oil 1000 mg daily (OTC)
- NO anticoagulant. NO antiplatelet (not on aspirin).

ALLERGIES: Sulfa drugs (maculopapular rash). No penicillin allergy.

FAMILY HISTORY: Father with atrial fibrillation and hypertension, died of an ischemic stroke at 74 (reportedly not anticoagulated). Mother with type 2 diabetes and CHF. Brother with CAD. (See family history for detail.)

SOCIAL HISTORY: Widowed 2025; lives alone in a two-story home in Towson; fully independent in all ADLs/IADLs and DRIVES HERSELF everywhere (highly values this autonomy). Daughter Karen lives ~20 minutes away, visits weekly, is the documented health-care proxy. Former smoker (~7 pack-years, quit 1994). Rare alcohol. Health-literate; keeps her own medication list and a home blood-pressure log.

ED COURSE / VITALS:
Arrival vitals: T 101.8 F, HR 108 (sinus tachycardia), BP 148/88, RR 20, SpO2 96% RA. Pain 8/10 RUQ.
- IV access, blood cultures x2, labs sent, IV fluids started.
- Preoperative 12-lead ECG (see IMG002): normal sinus rhythm (sinus tachycardia), no significant abnormalities, no ischemia.
- Portable CXR (IMG005): no acute cardiopulmonary process, normal heart size.
- CT abdomen/pelvis with contrast (IMG001): PERFORATED ACUTE CHOLECYSTITIS with large-volume pericholecystic fluid and localized peritonitis; cholelithiasis; no choledocholithiasis; no pancreatitis.
- Labs (LAB001): WBC 17.6 with neutrophilia; mild obstructive LFT pattern (T-bili 1.6, ALP 142); lipase normal (88); lactate 2.1; Cr 1.1; glucose 188; INR 1.1 (normal).
- Broad-spectrum antibiotics started: piperacillin-tazobactam 3.375 g IV.
- Acute Care Surgery (Dr. Vargas) evaluated: urgent OPEN cholecystectomy indicated given perforation + peritonitis.

PHYSICAL EXAMINATION:
General: Uncomfortable, ill-appearing woman in moderate distress from pain.
CV: Tachycardic, REGULAR rhythm. No murmur/rub/gallop. No peripheral edema. Pulses 2+.
Lungs: Clear bilaterally.
Abdomen: Tender to palpation in RUQ/epigastrium with voluntary guarding and a positive Murphy sign; focal rebound in the RUQ. Bowel sounds present.
Extremities: No edema, no calf tenderness.
Neuro: Alert and fully oriented; no focal deficit.

ASSESSMENT/PLAN:
72F with GERD, T2DM, and poorly-controlled HTN presenting with PERFORATED ACUTE CALCULOUS CHOLECYSTITIS and localized peritonitis.
#1 Perforated cholecystitis/peritonitis — broad-spectrum antibiotics (pip/tazo); urgent open cholecystectomy with Acute Care Surgery; NPO; IV fluids.
#2 T2DM — hold metformin perioperatively; sliding-scale insulin.
#3 HTN — hold lisinopril and HCTZ on day of surgery; continue amlodipine; monitor.
#4 VTE prophylaxis — mechanical (SCDs) preop; pharmacologic prophylaxis postop once hemostasis confirmed.
#5 Telemetry during perioperative period.
Code status: Full code (confirmed). Health-care proxy: daughter Karen Pierce-Walsh.

Documented by Dr. Anand (Medicine) with ED and Surgery input.
attestation: Electronically signed by Dr. Tomas Reyes, MD (Emergency Medicine); Dr. Priya Anand, MD (Internal Medicine PGY-2); co-signed Dr. Alan Whitfield, MD (Medicine attending) and Dr. Helena Vargas, MD (Acute Care Surgery). 6/10/2027.
